title: Take-rate simulation
summary: Run a take-rate (purchase-intent) simulation and return share, volume, and other measures per scenario × product.
description: Identical in computation to sim_run, but semantically intended for purchase-intent or take-rate analyses. In take-rate studies the sim_db respondent weights represent population estimates rather than sample weights, and the volume and revenue measures reflect projected market volume (units sold, revenue at price) rather than share-of-wallet. Use this command when the analytical goal is "how many units will be sold" rather than "what share of preferences does each product capture".
usage:
[sim_db]
excel = "model.xlsx"

[["cs::sim_take_rate"]]
scenarios = ["base"]
measures = "share|volume|revenue"
# optional:
subgroups = ["total"]
weights = ["w1"]
include_nets = false

arguments.name: measures
arguments.description: Pipe-separated output measures. For take-rate studies, volume and revenue are the most meaningful. Use "__all__" for all six measures.

arguments.name: scenarios
arguments.description: Names of scenarios to run. Use ["__all__"] for every scenario in the sim_db.

arguments.name: subgroups
arguments.description: Subgroup column names. Use ["__all__"] for every subgroup column.

arguments.name: weights
arguments.description: Weight column names. Use ["__all__"] for all weights. In take-rate studies weights are population projections.

arguments.name: throttles
arguments.description: Throttle-set name(s). Omit to use the sim_db default.

arguments.name: considerations
arguments.description: Consideration-set name(s). Omit to use the sim_db default.

arguments.name: include_nets
arguments.description: When true, append net rows summed by product_net group.

arguments.name: exponents
arguments.description: Beta scale factor applied before exponentiation.

arguments.name: prob_methods
arguments.description: Probability computation method(s). Single method or a pipe-separated blend (e.g. "sop|top2"). Methods: "sop" — standard share-of-preference (multinomial logit); "topN" where N ≥ 1 — top-N restricted MNL, only the N highest-utility products receive non-zero probability ("top1" is equivalent to first choice); "<top_spec>:<bot_spec>-<att>" — two-level nested logit where products are grouped by scenario attribute <att>, with <bot_spec> within groups and <top_spec> across groups (e.g. "sop:sop-Brand", "top1:sop-Category"); "<bot_spec>-<att>" shorthand sets top_spec=sop implicitly. Blend methods with | and weights via prob_methods_wgts.

arguments.name: trips
arguments.description: Monte Carlo shopping trips per respondent. Must be >= 1.

arguments.name: price_formulas
arguments.description: Eval-lang expressions for derived price attributes.

arguments.name: scenario_chgs
arguments.description: Attribute overrides keyed by product_name. For sim_take_rate, only the first value of any array is used.

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Rayon worker threads for the scenarios × weights fan-out. `1` (default) runs serially. Each (scenario, weight) pair is scored independently. Results are deterministic regardless of thread count.

details:
The simulator engine is identical to sim_run. The distinction is purely semantic: analysts use sim_take_rate to signal that the sim_db is configured for take-rate analysis (population-projected respondent weights, volume-based measures) rather than competitive share analysis.

All scenario_chgs and sweep features work identically to sim_run. The volume measure is computed as share × respondent weight (population projection); revenue as volume × price.

Population-projection volume requires that the sim_db respondent weights are calibrated to the target population size — typically annual purchase occasions or household counts.
value: Writes sim_take_rate_0.csv with all requested measures. In protocol mode emits event.run.started, event.artifact.created, event.run.finished.
notes: To sweep attribute values in a take-rate context, use sim_sensitivities with the same parameter surface.
Population-projection volume requires that the sim_db respondent weights are calibrated to the target population size.
standard_error reflects model uncertainty from HB draws, not sampling variability.

see_also.description: Multi-product attribute grid in take-rate context.

fields.name: scenarios
fields.description: Scenario name(s) to run. Use ["__all__"] for every scenario.

fields.name: subgroups
fields.description: Subgroup column name(s). Use ["__all__"] for all.

fields.name: weights
fields.description: Weight column name(s). Use ["__all__"] for all.

fields.name: throttles
fields.description: Throttle set name(s). Omit to use sim_db default.

fields.name: considerations
fields.description: Consideration set name(s). Omit to use sim_db default.

fields.name: include_nets
fields.description: Add net rows summed by product_net group. Default: false.

fields.name: exponents
fields.description: Beta scale factor applied before exponentiation. Default: 1.0.

fields.name: prob_methods
fields.description: Probability method: sop, topN (N≥1), nested <top>:<bot>-<att>, or pipe-blended. Default: sop.

fields.name: trips
fields.description: Monte Carlo shopping trips per respondent. Default: 1.

fields.name: trip_seeds
fields.description: Random seed for trip simulation. Default: 100.

fields.name: return_debug
fields.description: Include per-respondent debug output. Default: false.

fields.name: threads
fields.description: Rayon worker threads for scenarios × weights fan-out. Default: 1.
